data.title: The social media platform "X" appears to be experiencing a malfunction.
data.description: According to the internet monitoring website DownDetector, user reports show that as of 9:19 AM Eastern Time, 22,917 users in the United States had reported that the social media platform "X" was experiencing problems. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump Praises December Inflation Data, Suggests Fed Rate Cut
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, U.S. President Donald Trump has praised the December inflation data, suggesting that Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell should consider a rate cut.

data.title: Trump Media Technology Group Shares Rise After Launching New Investment Strategies
data.description: According to Odaily, shares of Trump Media Technology Group (DJT.O) increased by 1.7% in pre-market trading. This rise follows the company's introduction of four new investment strategies centered around the 'America First' theme.

data.title: DeFi protocol YO Protocol experienced an abnormal swap event on the Ethereum blockchain.
data.description: According to a post on the X platform by BlockSec, the DeFi protocol YO Protocol experienced an abnormal swap event on Ethereum, where users exchanged approximately $3.84 million worth of stkGHO for only about $122,000 worth of USDC. Analysis suggests the incident was caused by a combination of factors: 1) the initiator submitted an incorrect estimated output value, rendering slippage protection ineffective; 2) an abnormal transaction path configuration, passing through a high-fee, low-liquidity pool, resulting in significant fee extraction and price fluctuations. The team has since purchased GHO and re-deposited it into stkGHO in an attempt to mitigate the damage.

data.title: Trump praised the December inflation data, saying "Powell should cut interest rates significantly."
data.description: US President Trump: US (December) inflation data is out, and it's very low! This means Jerome Powell, "Too Late," should cut interest rates significantly! If he doesn't, he'll just continue to say "Too Late"! Furthermore, US economic growth data is also very good. Thank you, Mr. Tariff! (Jinshi)

data.title: Morgan Stanley Economist Comments on U.S. Inflation Trends
data.description: According to Odaily, Morgan Stanley Wealth Management's Chief Economic Strategist, Ellen Zentner, has commented on the current state of U.S. inflation. Zentner noted that the situation is reminiscent of past occurrences, with inflation not reigniting but still remaining above target levels. She highlighted that the cost transmission from tariffs remains limited, and there has been no improvement in housing affordability. Today's inflation report does not provide sufficient grounds for the Federal Reserve to consider a rate cut later this month.

data.title: Morgan Stanley: Inflation data insufficient to justify a Fed rate cut in January
data.description: Morgan Stanley Wealth Management Chief Economic Strategist Alan Zentner commented on US inflation: "We've seen this before. Inflation hasn't rebounded, but it remains above target. The cost pass-through from tariffs remains limited, but housing affordability hasn't improved. Today's inflation report is insufficient to provide the necessary justification for the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates later this month." (Jinshi)

data.title: An on-chain trader invested 293.26 BNB to trade "Dark Horse" and "Laozi," making a profit of $88,000.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, an on-chain trader with address 0x2b42 immediately entered the market after the "Dark Horse" and "Laozi" tokens were listed on Binance Alpha, investing 293.26 BNB (approximately $267,000) to purchase 44.78 million "Dark Horse" tokens and 5.61 million "Laozi" tokens. The trader subsequently sold all 44.78 million "Dark Horse" tokens and 366,799 "Laozi" tokens, obtaining 341.46 BNB (approximately $310,800), while still holding 5.24 million "Laozi" tokens worth $44,500. The total profit from this operation was approximately $88,000.

data.title: Fed mouthpiece: December CPI unlikely to change Fed's current wait-and-see attitude
data.description: Nick Timiraos, the Fed's mouthpiece, believes the December Consumer Price Index (CPI) is unlikely to change the Fed's current wait-and-see attitude, as officials are likely to want to see more evidence that inflation is stabilizing and gradually declining before cutting interest rates. The Fed has lowered its benchmark interest rate in its last three meetings, most recently in December, even though inflation stopped declining last year. Officials lowered rates due to concerns about a potentially larger-than-expected slowdown in the labor market. For Fed officials to resume rate cuts, they may need to see new evidence that labor market conditions are deteriorating or that price pressures are easing. The latter may require at least several more months of inflation data to become apparent. (Jinshi)

data.title: CME Group plans to launch 100-ounce silver futures contracts on February 9.
data.description: The CME Group announced on Tuesday its plan to launch a 100-ounce silver futures contract on February 9, 2026, pending regulatory approval. Jin Hennig, Managing Director and Global Head of Metals at CME Group, stated, “Amid rising geopolitical uncertainty and an accelerating energy transition, silver is increasingly favored by retail traders looking to diversify their portfolios across a wider range of metals. The launch of 100-ounce silver futures will enhance market accessibility, allowing more traders to benefit from the liquidity and efficiency offered by our futures markets.” JB Mackenzie, Vice President and General Manager of Futures and International Business at Robinhood Markets, noted, “This new futures contract from CME Group provides clients with a way to participate in silver trading with a lower capital threshold, offering traders greater flexibility.” (Jinshi)

data.title: IP prices broke through $3.30, with a more than 69% increase in 7 days.
data.description: According to OKX market data, IP has risen above $3.30, currently trading at $3.328, with a 24-hour increase of over 27% and a 7-day increase of over 69%, ranking among the top three gainers.

data.title: Strategists: If the inflation trend continues into December, the Fed will have room to cut rates in Q1.
data.description: Art Hogan, Chief Market Strategist at B. Riley Wealth, commented on the US CPI report: Today's CPI report brought some positive news, with December inflation performing more moderately than the market had previously expected. Overall CPI rose 2.7% year-on-year, in line with expectations; while core inflation was 2.6%, slightly lower than the market's original forecast of 2.7%. If this trend continues, it will provide the Federal Reserve with some policy leeway to cut interest rates in the first quarter. (Jinshi)

data.title: Goldman Sachs Prefers Long-Term Investment Themes Amid Inflation Data Release
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Alexandra Elissondo, Global Co-Chief Investment Officer of Multi-Asset Solutions at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, commented on the recently released CPI data, describing it as a welcome piece of hard data. However, she noted that as market concerns grow over the Federal Reserve's independence, inflation data may shift from being a primary market trigger to a background constraint. Goldman Sachs continues to favor long-term investment themes in risk assets, avoiding the pursuit of short-term news trends and instead focusing on sustainable and tradable topics.

data.description: On Jan 13, 2026, 14:01 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 92,000 USDT and is now trading at 91,979.257813 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.47% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Jump Crypto Transfers Significant Bitcoin Amount to Anonymous Address
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 21:51, Jump Crypto transferred 217.83 BTC to an anonymous address starting with 12YGEmHZ.

data.title: Strategist Highlights Impact of December CPI Data on Federal Reserve Expectations
data.description: According to Odaily, foreign exchange strategist Audrey Freeman has noted that the moderate Consumer Price Index (CPI) data for December has provided a welcome relief amid growing skepticism about the Federal Reserve's anticipated interest rate cuts. Freeman suggests that it may be premature to abandon expectations of the Federal Reserve adopting a dovish stance this year. Furthermore, she indicates that it might still be too early to dismiss the bearish outlook on the U.S. dollar driven by potential Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2026.

data.title: Gold and Silver Prices Rise Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description: According to PANews, spot gold prices have reached $4,620 per ounce, marking a 0.51% increase for the day. Meanwhile, spot silver has experienced a significant rise of over 3.00%, currently trading at $87.71 per ounce.

data.title: Traders Increase Bets on April Fed Rate Cut Amid Inflation Data
data.description: According to PANews, traders have intensified their bets that the Federal Reserve might lower interest rates before Fed Chair Jerome Powell's term ends in May. This shift follows a report from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics indicating that core consumer prices rose slightly less than anticipated. While traders still consider a rate cut in June as the most likely outcome, the probability of an April rate cut has increased to approximately 42%, up from 38% before the data release.
